MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-22-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DMITRI GODAMUNNE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

No appearsnces are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Godamunne's motion to set aside the default entered on June 2, 2021 is granted.

Defendant shows that the default was taken through inadvertence or excusable neglect by his counsel, who had scheduling issues due to her family staying longer over the Memorial Day weekend than expected. (Sakai Decl., par. 10.) Plaintiff fails to show any prejudice that would result from setting aside the default. "[T]he Supreme Court has recognized that if a defendant promptly seeks relief . . . and there is no showing of prejudice, 'very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside . . . unless inexcusable neglect is clear, the policy favoring trial on the merits prevails . . . Even if the mistake were caused by some negligence on defendant's part, this negligence might be excused if it in no way prejudiced the opposing party." (Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 696-697.) "[B]ecause the law strongly favors trial and disposition on the merits, any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default." (Murray & Murray v. Raissi Real Estate Development, LLC (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 379, 385.)

Further, Plaintiff's counsel failed to warn Defendant's counsel before taking the default. It is "well acknowledged that an attorney has an ethical obligation to warn opposing counsel that the attorney is about to take the adversary's default." (LaSalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135; accord, Fasuyi, 167 Cal.App.4th at 702.) Counsel had been in communication for months regarding the complaint, resulting in a stipulation by which Plaintiff's counsel agreed to file a first amended complaint-which he didn't serve until nearly two weeks after he filed it on April 15, 2021. (Sakai Decl. pars. 3-6.) Counsel agreed on June 1, 2021 as the date for Defendant to file a demurrer, yet the very next day, Plaintiff's counsel caused the default to be taken, less than one hour after speaking with Defendant's counsel. (Sakai Decl. par. 11.) Plaintiff's attempt to blame the Court's orders to show cause for his counsel's quiet speed in seeking a default is unavailing. In a response to the OSC filed on May 19, 2021, Plaintiff represented to the Court, "Plaintiff must file a request for entry of default within 10 days after the time for service is elapsed, which calculates to June 7, 2021." Yet Plaintiff's counsel caused the default to be taken five days earlier, when he knew that Defendant's counsel was planning imminently to file a motion. On these facts, it would be an abuse of discretion not to set aside the resulting default. (LaSalle, 36 Cal.App.5th at 137-140.)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2021 Continued To Sep-29-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Aug-02-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DMITRI GODAMUNNE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is Off calendar. Defendant Godamunne's default was entered on June 2, 2021. "The entry of a default terminates a defendant's rights to take any further affirmative steps in the litigation until either its default is set aside or a default judgment is entered. A defendant against whom a default has been entered is out of court and is not entitled to take any further steps in the cause affecting plaintiff's right of action; he cannot thereafter, until such default is set aside in a proper proceeding, file pleadings . . . ." (Devlin v. Kearny Mesa AMC/Jeep/Renault, Inc. (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 381, 385 (citations and internal quotations omitted).)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
